verses.text: I have lived 'in all good conscience'; the high priest orders his mouth smitten. Bold and struck. Honest words meet a violent answer.
I have lived 'in all good conscience'; the high priest orders his mouth smitten. Bold and struck. Honest words meet a violent answer.
'God shall smite thee, thou whited wall'; he knew not he was high priest, and would not revile the ruler. Quick humility. He owns the law's respect for office.
'God shall smite thee, thou whited wall'; he knew not he was high priest, and would not revile the ruler. Quick humility. He owns the law's respect for office.
'God shall smite thee, thou whited wall'; he knew not he was high priest, and would not revile the ruler. Quick humility. He owns the law's respect for office.
I am a Pharisee; for 'the hope and resurrection' I am questioned; the council divides. Shrewd appeal. He sets his accusers against one another.
I am a Pharisee; for 'the hope and resurrection' I am questioned; the council divides. Shrewd appeal. He sets his accusers against one another.
I am a Pharisee; for 'the hope and resurrection' I am questioned; the council divides. Shrewd appeal. He sets his accusers against one another.
A great dissension; the captain fears Paul be torn and takes him by force. Rescued again. God preserves him amid the strife.
A great dissension; the captain fears Paul be torn and takes him by force. Rescued again. God preserves him amid the strife.
The Lord stands by him, 'Be of good cheer... so must thou bear witness also at Rome.' Sweet assurance. Christ promises Paul shall reach Rome.
More than forty bind themselves by oath not to eat till they kill Paul. Deadly plot. Their hatred binds them to a desperate vow.
More than forty bind themselves by oath not to eat till they kill Paul. Deadly plot. Their hatred binds them to a desperate vow.
More than forty bind themselves by oath not to eat till they kill Paul. Deadly plot. Their hatred binds them to a desperate vow.
More than forty bind themselves by oath not to eat till they kill Paul. Deadly plot. Their hatred binds them to a desperate vow.
Paul's sister's son hears the plot and tells the captain. Providence. God uses a young kinsman to foil the scheme.
Paul's sister's son hears the plot and tells the captain. Providence. God uses a young kinsman to foil the scheme.
Paul's sister's son hears the plot and tells the captain. Providence. God uses a young kinsman to foil the scheme.
The captain learns the ambush and charges the lad to silence. Quiet wisdom. The plot is countered before it can strike.
The captain learns the ambush and charges the lad to silence. Quiet wisdom. The plot is countered before it can strike.
The captain learns the ambush and charges the lad to silence. Quiet wisdom. The plot is countered before it can strike.
The captain learns the ambush and charges the lad to silence. Quiet wisdom. The plot is countered before it can strike.
He prepares two hundred soldiers to bring Paul safely to Felix. Strong escort. God guards His servant with a Roman guard.
He prepares two hundred soldiers to bring Paul safely to Felix. Strong escort. God guards His servant with a Roman guard.
He writes that he rescued a Roman from the Jews. The record. The captain reports the case honestly.
He writes that he rescued a Roman from the Jews. The record. The captain reports the case honestly.
He writes that he rescued a Roman from the Jews. The record. The captain reports the case honestly.
Finding no charge worthy of death, he sends the accusers to Felix. No real crime. The law finds Paul innocent.
Finding no charge worthy of death, he sends the accusers to Felix. No real crime. The law finds Paul innocent.
Finding no charge worthy of death, he sends the accusers to Felix. No real crime. The law finds Paul innocent.
The soldiers bring Paul to Caesarea and deliver the letter. Safe arrival. The journey ends without harm.
The soldiers bring Paul to Caesarea and deliver the letter. Safe arrival. The journey ends without harm.
The soldiers bring Paul to Caesarea and deliver the letter. Safe arrival. The journey ends without harm.
Felix will hear him when his accusers come, keeping him in Herod's hall. Awaiting trial. The case is held for a proper hearing.
Felix will hear him when his accusers come, keeping him in Herod's hall. Awaiting trial. The case is held for a proper hearing.
